ud5.4:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud5.4:1.3: And on that occasion, a large number of boys on the road between Sāvatthī & Jeta’s Grove were catching fish.
ud5.4:2.1:
Then early in the morning the Blessed One adjusted his under robe and–
carrying his bowl & robes–went into Sāvatthī for alms.
ud5.4:2.2: He saw the large number of boys on the road between Sāvatthī & Jeta’s Grove catching little fish.
ud5.4:2.3: Seeing them, he went up to them and, on arrival, said to them,
ud5.4:2.4:
“Boys, do you fear pain?
Do you dislike pain?”
ud5.4:2.5:
“Yes, lord, we fear pain.
We dislike pain.”
ud5.4:3.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud5.4:4.3: don’t anywhere do an evil deed
ud5.4:5.1: If you’re doing or will do
ud5.4:5.4: catching up as you run away.
